description:
You are a front-end web-developer who has just enabled gzip compression on your
    staging server.  
    Before you merge anything to production, the build-pipeline needs a concise,
    machine-verifiable “compression report” that shows how well the JavaScript
    and CSS assets actually compress on disk.

    Your workspace already contains two asset files:

        1) /home/user/webapp/assets/main.js
        2) /home/user/webapp/assets/style.css

    Goal
    ----
    • Gzip-compress every “.js” and “.css” file that lives directly inside
      /home/user/webapp/assets (there are exactly the two files listed above).
    • Generate a tab-separated log named 
         /home/user/performance/compression_report.log
      that compares each original file with its freshly-created “.gz”.
    • Produce the log **strictly** in the format described below so an
      automated test can parse it.

    Log-file format (MUST match exactly)
    ------------------------------------
    • One line per processed file, followed by one summary line.
    • **Fields are separated by a single TAB character (ASCII 0x09).**
    • **Every line (including the last) terminates with a single “\n”.**
    • Numeric fields contain plain decimal integers except for the “savings
      percentage”, which is a fixed-point number with exactly two digits after the
      decimal point (e.g., 44.71).  
    • Field definitions per line:

        FIELD-1  Absolute path of the original file  
        FIELD-2  Size of the original file in bytes  
        FIELD-3  Size of the corresponding “.gz” file in bytes  
        FIELD-4  Space-savings percentage, computed as  
                 (1 – COMPRESSED_BYTES / ORIGINAL_BYTES) × 100  
                 and rounded to **exactly** two digits after the decimal point.

    • File lines must appear **in ascending alphabetical order** of their file
      name (i.e., main.js first, style.css second).
    • The summary line appears last and uses the literal word “TOTAL” in the
      first field.  Its remaining three fields hold the sums of columns 2 and 3
      and the overall savings percentage calculated from those sums.

    Example layout (numbers shown below are ONLY illustrative):

        /home/user/webapp/assets/main.js<TAB>3400<TAB>147<TAB>95.68\n
        /home/user/webapp/assets/style.css<TAB>3200<TAB>121<TAB>96.22\n
        TOTAL<TAB>6600<TAB>268<TAB>95.94\n

    Additional requirements
    -----------------------
    1. Create the directory /home/user/performance if it does not yet exist.
    2. Never delete or overwrite the original source files.
    3. The “.gz” files must be generated **in the same directory** as their
       originals and must retain the original base name plus the “.gz”
       extension (e.g., main.js.gz).
    4. Use only standard, CLI-available GNU/Linux tools (gzip, wc, stat, awk,
       printf, etc.).
    5. Make sure the log ends with a trailing newline so that commands such as
       “tail -n1” work correctly.

    Success criteria (what the autograder checks)
    ---------------------------------------------
    • /home/user/webapp/assets/main.js.gz exists.
    • /home/user/webapp/assets/style.css.gz exists.
    • /home/user/performance/compression_report.log exists.
    • The log has **exactly three** lines and follows the tab-separated format
      described above.
    • Column-2 values match the true byte-sizes of the originals
      (3,400 bytes for main.js and 3,200 bytes for style.css).
    • Column-3 values are strictly less than their respective originals.
    • Column-4 values are correct to two decimal places.
    • The TOTAL line contains the correct sums and percentage.

    Complexity limitation
    ---------------------
    A proficient engineer should be able to fulfil the above goals with roughly
    five to ten shell commands.  Please stay within that range when you craft
    your solution.
truth:
<!--
        PRE-EXISTING FILES (before the agent starts)

        1) /home/user/webapp/assets/main.js
           – 100 identical lines, each exactly:
             console.log('hello performance');\n
           – Each line is 34 bytes (ASCII).
           – Total file size: 100 × 34 = 3,400 bytes.

        2) /home/user/webapp/assets/style.css
           – 100 identical lines, each exactly:
             body { margin: 0; padding: 0; }\n
           – Each line is 32 bytes (ASCII).
           – Total file size: 100 × 32 = 3,200 bytes.

        Both files have filesystem permissions rw-r--r-- (0644) and are owned
        by user:user.

        The directory /home/user/performance does NOT yet exist; it is the
        agent’s responsibility to create it.

        EXPECTED ARTIFACTS (after the agent finishes)

        1) /home/user/webapp/assets/main.js.gz
        2) /home/user/webapp/assets/style.css.gz
        3) /home/user/performance/compression_report.log
           • Must contain exactly the three tab-separated lines described in
             the public task description.
           • Column-2 values must be “3400” and “3200”.
           • Column-3 values must be positive integers strictly less than
             column-2 of the same row.
           • Column-4 values must equal
               (1 – col-3 / col-2) × 100 ,
             rounded to two decimal places.
           • The TOTAL line must equal the sums of columns 2 and 3 and the
             percentage derived from those sums.
    -->
name: task_000000_f8ea72e8
